draft_content:
# How Many Leave Days Are Available to Employees?

## Overview

This article provides a clear answer to the common question: "How many leave days do I get?" It covers the standard annual leave entitlement for full-time and part-time employees at TechCorp, including how leave is accrued, carried over, and requested. Use this guide to understand your leave balance and plan your time off.

## Key Points

- **Full-time employees** receive **20 paid leave days per year** (pro-rated for part-time staff).
- Leave is accrued **monthly** (1.67 days per month for full-time employees).
- **Unused leave** can be carried over up to **5 days** into the next calendar year.
- **Public holidays** are separate from annual leave and do not count against your balance.
- **Sick leave** and **personal leave** are managed under different policies (see Related Resources).
- Leave requests must be approved by your manager at least **2 weeks in advance** (except for emergencies).

## Detailed Information

### Standard Annual Leave Entitlement

| Employment Type | Leave Days per Year | Accrual Rate (per month) |
|-----------------|---------------------|--------------------------|
| Full-time (40 hrs/week) | 20 days | 1.67 days |
| Part-time (20 hrs/week) | 10 days | 0.83 days |
| Part-time (other) | Pro-rated based on hours | Calculated per pay period |

- **New hires** accrue leave from their start date. You can take leave after completing your **probation period** (typically 3 months), but accrual begins immediately.
- **Leave year** runs from **January 1 to December 31**. Unused leave beyond the carry-over limit is forfeited unless otherwise approved by HR.

### How to Check Your Leave Balance

1. Log in to the **HR Portal** (hr.techcorp.com).
2. Navigate to **My Profile > Leave & Absence**.
3. Your current balance, accrued days, and pending requests are displayed.
4. For discrepancies, contact **HR Support** (hr@techcorp.com).

### Requesting Leave

- Submit requests via the **HR Portal** at least **14 calendar days** before the start date.
- Your manager will receive a notification and must approve or deny within **5 business days**.
- For **emergency leave** (e.g., sudden illness, family emergency), notify your manager by phone or email as soon as possible, then submit a request within 24 hours.

### Carry-Over and Payout

- **Carry-over**: Up to **5 unused days** can be transferred to the next year. Any excess is lost.
- **Payout upon termination**: Unused leave days are paid out at your current daily rate, up to a maximum of **20 days**.
- **No cash-out** for unused leave while employed (except in special circumstances approved by HR).

### Special Cases

- **Part-time employees**: Entitlement is calculated as (hours worked per week / 40) × 20 days.
- **Employees on parental leave**: Annual leave continues to accrue during statutory parental leave periods.
- **Long-service leave**: After **5 years** of continuous service, you receive an additional **5 days** of leave (one-time bonus).

## Actionable Takeaways

1. **Check your balance** monthly on the HR Portal to avoid losing unused days.
2. **Plan ahead** – submit leave requests at least 2 weeks in advance to ensure approval.
3. **Use or carry over** – if you have more than 5 unused days by November, schedule time off before year-end.
4. **Contact HR** if you have questions about your specific situation (e.g., part-time schedule, recent hire, or long-service bonus).

## Related Resources

- [Sick Leave Policy](https://hr.techcorp.com/policies/sick-leave)
- [Personal Leave Policy](https://hr.techcorp.com/policies/personal-leave)
- [Public Holidays Calendar](https://hr.techcorp.com/calendars/public-holidays)
- [Leave Request Guide (Step-by-Step)](https://hr.techcorp.com/guides/leave-request)
- [HR Support Contact](mailto:hr@techcorp.com) | Phone: ext. 4500

---

*Last updated: October 2023. For the most current information, always refer to the HR Portal or contact HR directly.*
